question: Who issued EO-2026-014A and at what date and time?
reference_answer: Sheriff Anthony Krasinski, on the recommendation of CAL FIRE IC Dale Frohlich, on April 14, 2026 at 18:30 PDT.

question: What is the order series number for this incident?
Who is the CAL FIRE incident commander?
reference_answer: Dale Frohlich.

question: What was the estimated acreage at packet time?
reference_answer: 4,820 acres at 3 percent containment.

question: Who filed the map correction MC-2026-014-01?
reference_answer: Lisette Boyer, GIS Analyst II, Tehama County.

question: What is the form number for the Sheriff's re-entry authorization?
reference_answer: Form TCSO-RE-04.

question: What wind speed and direction did the NWS spot forecast confirm?
reference_answer: 12 mph sustained wind from the southwest.

question: Who is the USFS Forest Supervisor on the email exchange?
reference_answer: Wesley Harlan.

question: Which source recorded the wind shift confirmation message at 03:42?
reference_answer: The TCSO dispatch radio log, Channel 4, 03:42 entry.

question: Which document records the boundary error correction?
reference_answer: Map correction MC-2026-014-01, filed 09:47 PDT April 15.

question: Which source records the eight vehicles stopped at the USFS gate at 07:35?
reference_answer: The TCSO dispatch radio log, Channel 4, 07:35 entry from Patrol 17 (Dep. Joaquin Reyes).

question: According to whose radio entry was the gate at MP 6 first reported as locked?
reference_answer: Patrol 17, Dep. Joaquin Reyes, in the radio log entry at 04:48 PDT.

question: Which GIS layer encodes road jurisdiction in this packet?
reference_answer: TCPW_Roads_v2025.4, the Tehama County Public Works road-jurisdiction layer.

question: Which document is the withdrawn pre-incident planning scenario?
reference_answer: EO-2026-014-DRAFT, the withdrawn April 13 pre-incident draft.

question: As of packet time, what evacuation order is in force for zone TC-7?
reference_answer: Evacuation Warning. EO-2026-014B downgraded TC-7 from Mandatory Evacuation to Evacuation Warning at 04:12 PDT April 15.

question: As of packet time, what is the current GIS layer of record?
reference_answer: TCSO_Evac_Layers_v1.1.1, published with the 09:47 correction.

question: Was the v1.1 GIS layer authoritative for the Manton Knolls subdivision parcels?
reference_answer: No. The v1.1 polygon erroneously placed Manton Knolls in TC-11; the authoritative state per v1.1.1 is TC-9. The v1.1 placement for those parcels is superseded.

question: As of packet time, what is the current evacuation order for the Manton Knolls subdivision?
reference_answer: MANDATORY EVACUATION as part of TC-9, per TCSO_Evac_Layers_v1.1.1.

question: Is EO-2026-014-DRAFT operative?
reference_answer: No. It was withdrawn on April 13, 2026 at the close of the tabletop exercise and is not operative.

question: As of 04:30 PDT on April 15, what evacuation order applied to the Manton Knolls subdivision according to the GIS layer published at that time?
reference_answer: According to the GIS layer published at that time (v1.1, published 04:12 PDT), Manton Knolls was placed in TC-11 with an Evacuation Warning. This was the published-but-erroneous status until the 09:51 correction.

question: As of 04:30 PDT on April 15, what evacuation order should have applied to the Manton Knolls subdivision according to the corrected understanding?
reference_answer: Mandatory Evacuation as part of TC-9. Per the v1.1.1 correction, this was always the intended order; the v1.1 placement was a drafting error.

question: As of packet time, has any re-entry authorization been signed for any zone?
reference_answer: No. As of packet time, no re-entry authorizations have been signed for any zone.

question: At 06:02 PDT on April 15, who returned the call regarding the USFS gate, and what did he state?
reference_answer: USFS Lassen LE Officer Patel returned the call. He stated the gate at MP 6 is a Forest Service jurisdictional matter and that he would consult the Forest Supervisor.

question: As of packet time, has the Tehama County Board of Supervisors voted on this incident?
reference_answer: No. The Board of Supervisors emergency briefing is scheduled for April 16 at 09:00 PDT, with no vote on the agenda.

question: Per the conditional re-entry framework, what conditions must be met before re-entry to TC-9 is authorized?
reference_answer: All four conditions: (1) damage assessment complete, (2) PG&E gas and electric restoration or confirmation of safe de-energized state, (3) hazard tree assessment complete, and (4) Sheriff's signed re-entry authorization on Form TCSO-RE-04.

question: Per the road-jurisdiction layer, who has authority over Forest Road 32N17?
reference_answer: Federal — USFS Lassen NF.

question: Per the road-jurisdiction layer, who maintains Highway Spur 36?
reference_answer: County — Tehama County Public Works (TCPW) maintained.

question: According to the packet, which source is the authoritative description of who controls the gate at MP 6 of Forest Road 32N17 — the radio log or the road-jurisdiction layer?
reference_answer: The road-jurisdiction layer TCPW_Roads_v2025.4. The packet states that where the radio log differs from the road-jurisdiction layer, the road-jurisdiction layer is the authoritative description of who controls the gate; the radio log describes events on the road.

question: If the Forest Supervisor's pending determination concludes USFS retains authority, what specific process must occur for civilian egress through the gate?
reference_answer: Not specified. The packet does not state the USFS process for opening the gate to civilian egress; the matter is pending Forest Supervisor and counsel determination.

question: How many evacuation zones are listed in EO-2026-014B?
reference_answer: Five zones: TC-7, TC-8, TC-9, TC-10, and TC-11.

question: How many parcels are affected by map correction MC-2026-014-01?
List all date-event anchors from §8 of the packet that occur on April 15, 2026 between 09:00 and 11:00 PDT.
reference_answer: Three: 09:47 PDT (map correction MC-2026-014-01 filed; v1.1.1 GIS layer published), 09:51 PDT (correction WEA alert sent), and 10:30 PDT (public information meeting comment period opened).

question: How many distinct WEA alerts have been sent regarding this incident as of packet time?
reference_answer: Three: 18:34 PDT April 14 (original), 04:18 PDT April 15 (revised), and 09:51 PDT April 15 (correction).

question: Has the question of authority over Forest Road 32N17 been determined?
reference_answer: No, not yet determined. Pending USFS Regional Office and counsel determination, with the Forest Supervisor expected to revert this afternoon.

question: Has County Counsel issued an opinion on whether the interim mapping error is an actionable notification deficiency under § 8588.10?
reference_answer: No. The question is referred to County Counsel and no opinion has been issued.

question: Has the IC decided whether to lift TC-7 to all-clear?
reference_answer: No. The decision is pending the IC's 17:00 PDT briefing.

question: Has the public information meeting comment period closed as of packet time (11:30 PDT)?
reference_answer: No. The comment period opened at 10:30 PDT and is scheduled to close at 17:00 PDT; packet time is 11:30 PDT, so the comment period is still open.

question: The radio log at 04:48 describes evacuees using Forest Road 32N17, while the road-jurisdiction layer marks the road as USFS. Does this constitute a conflict, and if so, how does the packet resolve it?
reference_answer: It is not a logical conflict. The radio log describes events on the road (evacuee use), and the road-jurisdiction layer describes who controls the gate (USFS). The packet resolves any apparent inconsistency by stating that the road-jurisdiction layer is authoritative on jurisdiction, while the radio log describes events.

question: Did the 04:18 WEA alert correctly describe the order applicable to Manton Knolls residents?
reference_answer: No. The 04:18 WEA alert reflected the v1.1 GIS layer, which placed Manton Knolls in TC-11 (Evacuation Warning). Per the v1.1.1 correction, the parcels should have been described as TC-9 Mandatory Evacuation. The 04:18 alert was incorrect for those parcels and was superseded by the 09:51 correction WEA.

question: The pre-incident draft assigned TC-7 to Evacuation Warning. EO-2026-014A assigned TC-7 to Mandatory Evacuation. EO-2026-014B downgraded TC-7 to Evacuation Warning. Which document is currently in force, and what was the operative order for TC-7 between 18:30 April 14 and 04:12 April 15?
reference_answer: EO-2026-014B is currently in force. EO-2026-014A was the operative order for TC-7 (Mandatory Evacuation) between 18:30 April 14 and 04:12 April 15, when it was superseded by EO-2026-014B (Evacuation Warning). EO-2026-014-DRAFT was withdrawn April 13 and was never operative.
